lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Jonah went out east of the city and made for himself a place to watch what would become of Nineveh.

lines.speaker: Jonah
lines.text: One more piece will make a little shade.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The shelter finally stands. Thin shade, but enough for waiting.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The LORD appointed a plant and made it come up over Jonah, that it might be a shade over his head.

lines.speaker: Jonah
lines.text: At last, something gentle in the heat.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: At dawn the next day, God appointed a worm that attacked the plant, so that it withered.

lines.speaker: Jonah
lines.text: Shade gone. Stem split. Dust and sun again.

lines.speaker: Voice
lines.text: Before the last question is spoken to Jonah, remember what the LORD has shown him.

lines.speaker: LORD
lines.text: You pity the plant, for which you did not labor, nor did you make it grow.

lines.speaker: LORD
lines.text: Should I not pity Nineveh, that great city, with more than one hundred twenty thousand persons who do not know their right hand from their left?
